MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues
Off Calendar Sep-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Kevin P. Martin of Martin APC (510-444-7600) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Darren Lee

Attorney, Betty M. Kwan, Esq. (626-285-1123) appears as counsel for cross-defendant/moving party, Gloria Wong

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Moving party counsel's oral evidentiary objections are heard as set forth in the transcript. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

CROSS-DEFENDANT GLORIA WONG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues regarding the cross-claims of Darren Lee and Suzanne Lee is DENIED in its entirety. As to issue 1, Ms. Wong failed to meet her burden of production and, even if she did, the Lees presented evidence which raises a disputed issue whether Ms. Wong was interfering with their prospective business. As to issue 2, the Lees presented evidence which raises a disputed issue whether Ms. Lee was a member of New On Sang, LLC, and whether Ms. Wong had knowledge of Ms. Lee's membership status. Summary adjudication of fourth cause of action on the grounds that Mr. Lee was required to file a derivative action is denied. As noted in PacLink Communications, Intern, Inc. v. Superior Court (2001) 90 Cal.App.4th 958, 964, a member may bring an individual action, without also bringing a derivative action, if he has a direct ownership interest in the corporation's assets. Ms. Wong has not presented evidence which shows that the Lees do not have a direct ownership interest in New On Sang, LLC's assets. Summary adjudication of the first through twelfth causes of action on the grounds of waiver, release, and estoppel is denied. The mere recital of a waiver of the protection of Civil Code 1542, or that the release covers unknown claims or unknown parties is not controlling. Whether the releaser intended to discharge such claims or parties is a question of fact. (Leaf v. City of San Mateo (1980) 104 Cal.App.3d 398.) Ms. Wong has not presented evidence which establishes that the claims were waived or released. The cross-complaint includes allegations regarding events that occurred after the settlement was entered into and Ms. Wong does not demonstrate how these claims were released by the settlement. Moreover, the evidence which Ms. Wong presents fails to establish that there are no disputed issues whether the events which provide the basis of the Lees' claims were known or should have been known by them when they signed the release. Summary adjudication of the first through twelfth causes of action on the grounds that the claims are barred by the statute of limitations and the affirmative defense of unclean hands is denied because Ms. Wong failed to meet her burden of production. Summary adjudication of the eighth, tenth, eleventh, and twelfth causes of action on the grounds that the trademark and tradename have not been used is denied for the same reason. Summary adjudication of the ninth and twelfth causes of action on the grounds that Ms. Wong has not used the trade secret, reasonable discovery of the trade secret, and lack of knowledge of the trade secret is denied because Ms. Wong's declaration and Exhibits O, L, S, and V do not establish that there are no disputed issues whether Ms. Wong improperly utilized cross-complainants' trade secrets. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
